title: Melted German Cabbage Stew
description: An earthy, cheese‑pull pork‑free stew that soothes hangovers, featuring Chinese cabbage, a mirepoix base, and a generous melt of Emmental.
instructions: Chop the onion, carrot, celery, and Chinese cabbage into bite‑sized pieces.
Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat and sauté the onion, carrot, and celery until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped cabbage and sauté for another 3 minutes until it begins to wilt.
Pour in the vegetable broth, add salt, pepper, thyme, bay leaf, and mustard powder, and bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let the stew simmer, uncovered, for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Remove the bay leaf and stir in the grated Emmental cheese until it fully melts into the stew.
Taste and adjust seasoning with additional salt or pepper if needed.
Ladle the hot stew into bowls and serve immediately, optionally with crusty bread on the side.
